revision.text:
[[ja:йҳҝй ҲиіҖзҘһзӨҫ]] {{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|йЈӣйіҘеқҗзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}|qq=}} , that page was originally under this title due to a mistranslation.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:55, 26 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} {{infobox religious building |name=Asuka Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japan01n.jpg |address=[[Asuka, ShingЕ« City, Wakayama Prefecture|1-25-25, Asuka, ShingЕ«, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan]] |coordinates={{Coord|33.728475|N|135.99729194444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|зҶҠйҮҺжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}
Kumano Hayatama no ЕҢkami, Kumano Fusumi no ЕҢkami, Ketsumi Miko no ЕҢkami |Sacred Object= |type=Former [[Village Shrine]] |founding=Unspecified (KЕҚshЕҚ Emperor's era) |Main Hall=Wooden, copper-plated roof [[Nagare-zukuri]] |Pilgrimage Site=Kumano Mandala No. 23 |festival=October 15 (Rite of the Sacred Horse Parade) |Rituals= |map= {{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Asuka Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|qq=}} |prefecture={{ill|Shingu|qid=Q823526|1=ShingЕ«, Wakayama}} }} '''Asuka Shrine''' (йҳҝй ҲиіҖзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|en|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|qid=Q823526|lt=ShingЕ«, Wakayama Prefecture|1=ShingЕ«, Wakayama}}, Japan. It is the 23rd site in the {{ill|Kumano Mandala Thirty-three Sacred Places|ja|зҶҠйҮҺжӣјиҚјзҫ…дёүеҚҒдёүгғ¶жүҖйңҠе ҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Mandala Thirty-three Sacred Places|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502342|1=Kumano Mandala Thirty-three Sacred Places}}. == History and Origins == Asuka Shrine is situated on the southern slope of a small hill called Mount HЕҚrai, near the mouth of the {{ill|Kumano River|en|Kumano River|qid=Q1972071|lt=Kumano River|1=Kumano River}}. The hill, measuring 100 meters from north to south and 50 meters from east to west, has an iconic shape reminiscent of an inverted bowl, typical of {{ill|Kannabi|en|Kannabi|qid=Q11588874|lt=Sacred mountains (Kanabi)|1=Kannabi}}.ShingЕ« City Education Board & ShingЕ« Cultural Properties Council [1990: 30] Historical records, such as the "ShingЕ« Main Shrine Sub-shrine Comprehensive Catalog," indicate that this location was a site of ancient worship, as evidenced by the presence of ritual sites like Kami-no-Tomokura and Shimo-no-Tomokura. Furthermore, some theories suggest it was once an island separated from the mainland.[[#toyoshima|иұҠеі¶дҝ® (1992), p.56]] In Kumano belief, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|зҶҠйҮҺжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was said to have first descended at {{ill|Kamikura Shrine|ja|зҘһеҖүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikura Shrine|qq=}} before being enshrined 61 years later at a valley north of Asuka Shrine, known as Iwabuchi Valley.Identified today as the site of {{ill|Kinetsuya Shrine|ja|иІҙзҰ°и°·зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinetsuya Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|KihЕҚ|en|KihЕҚ|qid=Q1203117|lt=KihЕҚ, Mie Prefecture|1=KihЕҚ}}. [Heibonsha 1997: 221] This valley is considered the origin of the deityвҖҷs formal name, Ketsumi Miko, as noted in the "Kumano Gongen Manifestation Origins."Heibonsha [1997: 53] The shrine's origins reportedly date back to Emperor KЕҚshЕҚвҖҷs era, according to tradition. During the late Heian period, Asuka Shrine became integral to Kumano pilgrimages. It was regarded as one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|lt=Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|lt=Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|ja|зҶҠйҮҺд№қеҚҒд№қзҺӢеӯҗ|lt=Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|lt=Ninety-Nine ЕҢji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to д№қеҚҒд№қзҺӢеӯҗ|1=Ninety-Nine ЕҢji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}} (Ninety-Nine Kumano ЕҢji Shrines) and referenced in historical records like the "{{ill|ChЕ«yЕ«ki|ja|дёӯеҸіиЁҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕ«yЕ«ki|qq=}}" (dd09 CE).{{ill|ChЕ«yЕ«ki|ja|дёӯеҸіиЁҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕ«yЕ«ki|qq=}}, Tennin 2, October 27 entry During the Meiji period, smaller shrines like Yatagarasu Shrine and Miyado Shrine were merged into Asuka Shrine. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|зҶҠйҮҺжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} (дәӢи§Јз”·е‘Ҫ) * Kumano Hayatama no ЕҢkami (зҶҠйҮҺйҖҹзҺүеӨ§зҘһ), Kumano Fusumi no ЕҢkami (зҶҠйҮҺеӨ«й ҲзҫҺеӨ§зҘһ), Ketsumi Miko no ЕҢkami (е®¶жҙҘзҫҺеҫЎеӯҗеӨ§зҘһ) === Additional Deities === * Taketsune no Kami (дәӢи§’иҰӢе‘Ҫ) * {{ill|Yomotsu Michi no Kami|ja|й»„жіүжҙҘйҒ“е®Ҳе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Michi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Shrine Grounds == === Structures === [[File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japan06n.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] * Main Hall (Wooden, copper-plated roof, Nagare-zukuri style) вҖ“ 16 square meters * Worship Hall (Wooden, copper-plated roof, Irimoya-zukuri style) вҖ“ 56 square meters === Sub-Shrines === * Asuka Inari Shrine (Wooden, copper-shingle roof) вҖ“ 4 square meters * Jofuku-no-Miya (Stone construction) вҖ“ 0.39 square meters * Koyasu-no-Sha File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japan10n.jpg|Asuka Inari Shrine File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japanddn.jpg|Jofuku-no-Miya File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japan09n.jpg|Koyasu-no-Sha == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Artifacts excavated from Asuka Shrine grounds, including over 200 items such as copper mirrors, bronze Buddhist statue fragments, and Heian-era one-character-per-stone scriptures. These items are preserved at the {{ill|ShingЕ« Municipal Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮз«ӢжӯҙеҸІж°‘дҝ—иіҮж–ҷйӨЁ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShingЕ« Municipal Historical and Folklore Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Government Gazette, July 23, 2019. === Asuka Ruins === This archaeological site includes Yayoi-period pit dwellings and evidence of a large settlement that prospered along the Kumano River estuary. Excavated items include Yayoi pottery, Haji pottery, and ornamental stones.ShingЕ« City Education Board & ShingЕ« Cultural Properties Council [1990: 81] == ShingЕ« Municipal Historical and Folklore Museum == {{Museum |name=ShingЕ« Municipal Historical and Folklore Museum | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:171008 Asuka-jinja Shingu Wakayama pref Japan13n.jpg|270px]] |Specialization=History and folklore of ShingЕ« City |Established=1979 |address=1-25-25, Asuka, ShingЕ«, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan }} The museum houses artifacts from the shrine, the Kumano faith, and historical displays, including a reconstructed Yayoi pit dwelling from 1976. == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|ShingЕ« Station|en|ShingЕ« Station|qid=Q5357415|lt=ShingЕ« Station|1=ShingЕ« Station}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|qid=Q582803|lt=JR Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamikura Shrine|ja|зҘһеҖүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikura Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qid=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} * {{ill|Kinetsuya Shrine|ja|иІҙзҰ°и°·зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinetsuya Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.shingu.wakayama.jp/ ShingЕ« City Historical Museum] (Official Website) * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=8002 Wakayama Shrine Association вҖ“ Asuka Shrine] {{translated page|ja|йҳҝй ҲиіҖзҘһзӨҫ|version=102989605|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:йҳҝй ҲиіҖзҘһзӨҫ]] {{Commons category|Asuka-jinja (ShingЕ«, Wakayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q11658011}} properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:ж–°е®®еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:ж–°е®®еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]
